yesterdayAnalysis: Completed the planned 1,500m open water swim at La Jolla Shores (39 TSS), providing excellent race-specific practice 29 days out from IM 70.3 Oregon. This was the Friday OWS component paired with the easy run. Sleep quality after this session was poor (score 48), with elevated RHR (57 vs 50-54 range), minimal REM (37min), and reduced SpO2 (93%).
todayRecommendation: Execute today's long ride (50-55mi, NP <150w) and brick run, but monitor carefully given last night's poor sleep recovery. Consider shortening to 50mi if fatigue is evident, prioritizing quality pacing over distance. The brick run discipline remains critical with 10 completed so far.
weekProgress: Week 9 of 10 is complete at 374 of 320 TSS target (117%). All scheduled sessions executed successfully through Friday including two brick workouts (Mon, Wed), two quality swims (Tue, Thu), the Friday easy run + OWS, and today's long ride completes the week. Volume properly stepped down by ~35% as planned for transition phase.
keyInsight: Poor sleep recovery (48 score) following yesterday's OWS with elevated RHR and minimal REM suggests accumulated fatigue going into today's long ride — monitor closely and prioritize conservative pacing over distance completion.
